key_insight: A transformer-based flow matching model can generate path-dependent plastic stress fields in one step by operating in a VAE latent space and treating the time evolution as video synthesis.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast surrogate for costly FEM simulations of nonlinear, geometry-dependent loading paths, especially for high-resolution studies where repeated time stepping is expensive.
method: FM model with transformer backbone in latent VAE space
Generates full stress-field trajectories across all time steps as a video-like sequence
Uses non-Gaussian source distribution, token-level loading embeddings, and auxiliary networks to reduce path crossings and improve conditioning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate high-resolution generation from a limited dataset and speedups of 6–7× over CPU FEM and about two orders of magnitude on consumer GPUs.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful training of the latent representation, loading embeddings, and non-Gaussian transport design, so generalization to unseen material laws or loading regimes should be validated.
paper_title: One-Step Flow Matching for Generative Modeling of Path-Dependent Physical Fields
